title: मङ्गल ग्रह कवचम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री मङ्गल ग्रह कवचम् ॥

॥ श्री गणेशाय नमः ॥

अस्य श्रीमङ्गलकवचस्य विश्वामित्र ऋषिः।
अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। मङ्गलो देवता।
मङ्गलप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

धरणीगर्भसम्भूतं विद्युत्कान्तिसमप्रभम्।
कुमारं शक्तिहस्तं तं मङ्गलं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ १॥

मङ्गलः शिरो रक्षेत् ललाटं लोहिताङ्गकः।
नेत्रे रक्तवपुः पातु कर्णौ भूमिसुतोऽवतु॥ २॥

नासां रक्षतु वक्राङ्गो मुखं मे रक्तलोचनः।
जिह्वां कुजः सदा रक्षेत् कण्ठं मे भूमिनन्दनः॥ ३॥

भुजौ महीसुतः पातु करौ रक्षतु मेषभः।
हृदयं पातु भौमो मे कुक्षिं रिणहरो रक्षतु॥ ४॥

नाभिं रक्षतु मे कुजो मध्यं पातु महाबलः।
कटिं धरासुतः पातु ऊरू रक्षतु क्रूरदृक्॥ ५॥

जानुनी अङ्गारकः पातु जङ्घे रक्षतु रक्तकः।
पादौ भूमिजः पातु सर्वाङ्गं मङ्गलो रक्षतु॥ ६॥

य एतत्कवचं दिव्यं मङ्गलस्य सदा पठेत्।
मङ्गलवारे विशेषेण सर्वपीडा विनश्यति॥ ७॥

भूमिलाभो भवेत्तस्य शत्रुनाशो भवेद्ध्रुवम्।
रोगनाशो भवेन्नित्यं मङ्गलस्य प्रसादतः॥ ८॥

॥ इति श्री मङ्गल ग्रह कवचम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Mangal Graha Kavacham ||

|| Shri Ganeshaya Namah ||

Asya Shri Mangala Kavachasya Vishvamitra Rishih |
Anushtup Chhandah | Mangalo Devata |
Mangala Prityarthe Jape Viniyogah ||

Dharanigarbha Sambhutam Vidyutkanti Samaprabham |
Kumaram Shaktihastam Tam Mangalam Pranamamy Aham || 1 ||

Mangalah Shiro Rakshet Lalatam Lohitangakah |
Netre Raktavapuh Patu Karnau Bhumisuto'vatu || 2 ||

Nasam Rakshatu Vakrango Mukham Me Raktalocanah |
Jihvam Kujah Sada Rakshet Kantham Me Bhuminandanah || 3 ||

Bhujau Mahisutah Patu Karau Rakshatu Meshabhah |
Hridayam Patu Bhaumo Me Kukshim Rinaharo Rakshatu || 4 ||

Nabhim Rakshatu Me Kujo Madhyam Patu Mahabalah |
Katim Dharasutah Patu Uru Rakshatu Kruradrik || 5 ||

Januni Angarakah Patu Janghe Rakshatu Raktakah |
Padau Bhumijah Patu Sarvangam Mangalo Rakshatu || 6 ||

Ya Etat Kavacham Divyam Mangalasya Sada Pathet |
Mangalavare Visheshena Sarva Pida Vinashyati || 7 ||

Bhumi Labho Bhaved Tasya Shatrunasho Bhaved Dhruvam |
Roganasho Bhaven Nityam Mangalasya Prasadatah || 8 ||

|| Iti Shri Mangal Graha Kavacham Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
पृथ्वी के गर्भ से उत्पन्न, विद्युत् के समान कान्ति वाले, कुमार (युवा), शक्ति (भाला) हस्त में धारण किए मंगल ग्रह को प्रणाम।

मंगल मस्तक की, लोहितांग (लाल शरीर वाला) ललाट की, रक्तवपु नेत्रों की, भूमिसुत कानों की रक्षा करें। वक्रांग नासिका की, रक्तलोचन मुख की, कुज जिह्वा की, भूमिनन्दन कण्ठ की रक्षा करें।

महीसुत भुजाओं की, मेषभ (मेष राशि का स्वामी) हाथों की, भौम हृदय की, ऋणहर उदर की रक्षा करें। कुज नाभि की, महाबल मध्य भाग की, धरासुत कटि की, क्रूरदृक् ऊरुओं की रक्षा करें। अंगारक घुटनों की, रक्तक जंघाओं की, भूमिज पैरों की, मंगल सर्वांग की रक्षा करें।

जो इस मंगल कवच का नित्य पाठ करता है, विशेषकर मंगलवार को, उसकी सर्व पीड़ा नष्ट होती है। भूमि लाभ, शत्रुनाश और रोगनाश होता है।
benefits: Protective kavach for Mars (Mangal/Kuja) related afflictions
Traditionally recited for removal of mangal dosha effects in horoscope
Invokes land, property and real estate benefits
Traditionally recited for removal of enemies and removes disease
Recite on Tuesday for maximum Mars-related benefits
description: Dedicated to the Nine Planets (Navagraha), the Mangal Graha Kavacham (मङ्गल ग्रह कवचम्) is a divine armor prayer for protection revered in Hindu tradition. This sacred composition is sourced from Graha Stotra Sangraha and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Devotees recite this prayer to protective kavach for Mars (Mangal/Kuja) related afflictions and destroy enemies and remove disease. It is also said to recite on Tuesday for maximum Mars-related benefits. This prayer is traditionally recited on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and during Mangal Jayanti to invoke divine grace. Comprising 8 verses, it can be completed in approximately 4 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Mangal Graha Kavacham in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Mangal Graha Kavacham in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Mars protection kavach for Mangal Dosha, property gains and enemy destruction.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Mangal Graha Kavacham and how does it differ from the Mangal Kavacham?
seoFaqs.answer: The Mangal Graha Kavacham is a Sanskrit kavacha (armor) stotra of approximately eight verses dedicated to Mangal — the planet Mars, third of the Navagraha — preserved in the Graha Stotra Sangraha tradition. The phrase Mangal Graha (literally Mangal the planet) explicitly frames the deity as a graha-devata in the Vedic-astrological context, focusing on planetary remedy. The companion text Mangal Kavacham, preserved in the Brahmanda Purana, has a slightly different verse structure and tends to invoke Mars more in the warrior-deity aspect (as the son of the Earth, holding shakti and gada). Both kavachas serve as armor against Mars-related afflictions; many practitioners recite both for complete coverage. (Sources: Graha Stotra Sangraha; Brahmanda Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Mangal in Vedic astrology and the Puranas?
seoFaqs.answer: Mangal (also Bhauma, Kuja, Angaraka, Mahisuta) is the Vedic deity of the planet Mars, born of the Earth (Bhumi) and so called Bhauma or Kuja (kuh meaning earth). The puranic narrative in the Skanda Purana describes him as born from a drop of Shiva's sweat that fell on the earth during his tapas, making him an aditya-amsha of Shiva himself. Astrologically Mars governs courage (parakrama), military and police service, surgery, real estate, brothers (especially younger), discipline, anger, blood and inflammation, and accidents. He owns the rashis Mesha (Aries) and Vrishchika (Scorpio), is exalted in Makara (Capricorn) and debilitated in Karka (Cancer). His weekday is Tuesday (Mangalvar), colour is red, gemstone is red coral, and direction is south. (Sources: Skanda Purana Tirthamahatmya; Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra.)

seoFaqs.question: What is Mangal dosha and when does it occur?
seoFaqs.answer: Mangal dosha (also called Manglik dosha or Kuja dosha) is a classical astrological condition in which Mars occupies the first, second, fourth, seventh, eighth, or twelfth house of the natal chart counted from the lagna (some traditions also count from the Moon and from Venus). When this configuration is present at the time of birth, it is traditionally believed to delay marriage or create friction in the spouse-relationship, particularly in the early years. However, classical commentators including Mantreshvara in Phaladeepika and Varahamihira in Brihat Jataka enumerate over a hundred parihara (cancellation) yogas — the dosha is cancelled if both partners are manglik, if the dosha is in chara rashis, if Mars aspects Jupiter, and several other combinations. A proper analysis by a qualified Vedic astrologer is essential before attributing marital problems to Mangal alone. (Sources: Phaladeepika; Brihat Jataka; Jataka Parijata.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Mangal Graha Kavacham be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Mangal Graha Kavacham is most powerful when recited on Tuesdays (Mangalvar — the day of Mars), in the morning during the Mars hora (planetary hour), facing south or south-east, with the devotee dressed in red or saffron and offering red flowers (hibiscus, kanakambaram), red sandalwood paste, jaggery, masoor dal and red cloth at a clay or copper pot representing the deity. Other potent occasions are Mangalvar of Shukla paksha during Margashirsha or Pausha months, Hanuman Jayanti (since Hanuman and Mangal share warrior characteristics), Skanda Shashti (since Mars is connected to Kartikeya's army), and during one's running Mars dasha or antardasha. Standard japa is 108 recitations of the kavacha or its accompanying beej mantra. (Sources: Graha-shanti vidhi; Lal Kitab; Parashari remedial astrology.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Mangal beej mantra and how many times should it be chanted?
seoFaqs.answer: The classical Mangal beej mantra is Om Kraam Kreem Kraum Sah Bhaumaya Namah (the seed-formula encoded around Bha — the seed of earth). The simpler one used in many kavacha viniyogas is Om Ang Angarakaya Namah. The Vedic Mars mantra is Om Agni Murdha Divah Kakut Patih Prithivya Ayam. Apastambha Sutra prescribes 7,000 to 10,000 repetitions of the beej mantra over forty days (one mandala) to constitute a complete Mangal shanti japa, ideally accompanied by donation of red lentils (masur), jaggery, copper, red cloth and red coral on Tuesday. The recitation is undertaken with rudraksha or red coral mala after sankalpa stating the specific intention of pacifying Mars in the natal chart. (Sources: Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra; standard graha-japa manuals.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Mangal Graha Kavacham bestow?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional commentaries and the closing phalashruti of the kavacham promise that sincere recitation provides a divine armor against all Mars-related afflictions, removes mangal dosha effects from the natal chart and remediates manglik issues delaying marriage, grants bhumi-labha (gain of land and real estate, since Mars is bhauma — son of Earth), success in litigation especially property disputes, removal of debts and financial burdens caused by Mars, victory over enemies and competitors, courage and parakrama in the face of obstacles, protection from accidents, surgical complications and blood disorders, and overall vital energy (ojas) and discipline. For those in military, police, surgery, real estate or athletics it bestows excellence in their field. (Sources: Mangal Graha Kavacham phalashruti; Graha Stotra Sangraha.)
